commands.author: litang9

target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Public Contract Rationale

- `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` is optional and additive. Existing providers with quota windows keep using `windows`; shared formatters only fall back to `summary` when a provider has no windows and no error.
- `models.authStatus.usage.summary` is optional and mirrors the same snapshot field so API-key balance providers can expose account-state text without changing existing window payloads.
- The provider-usage SDK export keeps DeepSeek-specific HTTP parsing inside the bundled DeepSeek provider path instead of requiring plugins to import internal `src` modules.
- Tests cover summary-only formatting, DeepSeek auth-status enrichment, provider loading, and existing window-based formatting behavior.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `0c4e71c19fd7f8a23ed0a6fb8ba8e095889b3b4e`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=0c4e71c19fd7f8a23ed0a6fb8ba8e095889b3b4e
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance <redacted>
formatted=Balance <redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence: Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- GitHub CI is green on latest head `0c4e71c19fd7f8a23ed0a6fb8ba8e095889b3b4e`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/gateway/gateway-acp-spawn-defaults.live.test.ts src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `git diff --check`

## Current Branch Note

This branch is refreshed onto current `main` at `5b310a7b27fa55dac0ab368f3d1372b7a17617dc` and is mergeable. Latest follow-ups align subagent config typing, clarify usage docs, aggregate every parseable DeepSeek balance entry, refresh the plugin SDK API baseline, and fix current-main CI drift in Vite hook typing, dynamic alias deadcode scanning, bash-tools shell snapshot tests, ACP live-test shard snapshots, ACP spawn-defaults lint, and provider stream cleanup tests.
